+Users with ADHD suffer from time blindness — they step away briefly and lose an hour. A simple clock-in/clock-out toggle with a live elapsed timer and persisted daily totals gives concrete feedback about time actually worked. Historical data (time per day) is stored for future display.
-**What:** Completed tasks appear in a collapsible "Done" section at the bottom of the task list instead of disappearing.
+  date       TEXT    NOT NULL,              -- ISO date "YYYY-MM-DD"
+  started_at TEXT    NOT NULL,              -- ISO datetime
+  ended_at   TEXT                           -- NULL while session is active

-- Done tasks are read-only (no drag handles, no complete button — already complete); keep edit/delete actions
-- Done tasks show `completedAt` date instead of due date metadata
-## Feature 2: Descriptions on tasks and subtasks
-**What:** Optional freeform text description field on both tasks and subtasks.

-- XpBar section: wrap in a container with a clickable header row (`▼ Level` / `▶ Level`) that toggles `levelCollapsed`; conditionally render `<XpBar />`
-- Categories section: same pattern — clickable `▼ Categories` / `▶ Categories` header toggles `categoriesCollapsed`; conditionally render category list

# Plan: ADHD Todo — Three Feature Additions

## Context
Three items from the project's `to-do.md`:
1. Completed tasks should persist in a visible "Done" section
2. Tasks and subtasks should support an optional description field
3. All sections in the left sidebar (Level/XP bar, Categories) should be individually collapsible

---

## Feature 1: "Done" section in task list

**What:** Completed tasks appear in a collapsible "Done" section at the bottom of the task list instead of disappearing.

**How:**
- `App.tsx`: Pass `includeComplete: true` to `useTasks()` — infrastructure already exists in DB/IPC/hook
- `App.tsx`: Split `tasks` into `activeTasks` (incomplete) and `doneTasks` (complete) before passing to `TaskList`
- `TaskList.tsx`: Accept separate `doneTasks` prop; render them below active tasks under a collapsible "Done (N)" header (local `useState` for collapsed)
- Done tasks are read-only (no drag handles, no complete button — already complete); keep edit/delete actions
- Done tasks show `completedAt` date instead of due date metadata

**Files:** `src/renderer/src/App.tsx`, `src/renderer/src/components/tasks/TaskList.tsx`

---

## Feature 2: Descriptions on tasks and subtasks

**What:** Optional freeform text description field on both tasks and subtasks.

**How:**

### DB + types
- `migrations.ts`: Two new `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN description TEXT` statements in `try/catch` blocks (matching existing xp_value pattern)
- `types.ts`: Add `description: string | null` to `Task` and `Subtask`; update `CreateSubtaskPayload` to include optional `description`

### Backend
- `db/tasks.ts`: Add `description` to `SELECT`, `INSERT`, and `UPDATE` queries
- `db/subtasks.ts`: Add `description` to `SELECT`, `INSERT` queries; add update support

### Frontend
- `TaskForm.tsx`: Add a `<textarea>` for description below the title field
- `SubtaskList.tsx`: Expand subtask row to show/edit inline description (small italic line below title, click to edit)

**Files:** `src/main/db/migrations.ts`, `src/shared/types.ts`, `src/main/db/tasks.ts`, `src/main/db/subtasks.ts`, `src/main/ipc/subtasks.ts`, `src/renderer/src/components/tasks/TaskForm.tsx`, `src/renderer/src/components/tasks/SubtaskList.tsx`

---

## Feature 3: Collapsible sidebar sections

**What:** Level/XP bar and Categories nav each have a collapse toggle in their headers.

**How:**
- `Sidebar.tsx`: Add two `useState` booleans — `levelCollapsed` and `categoriesCollapsed`
- XpBar section: wrap in a container with a clickable header row (`▼ Level` / `▶ Level`) that toggles `levelCollapsed`; conditionally render `<XpBar />`
- Categories section: same pattern — clickable `▼ Categories` / `▶ Categories` header toggles `categoriesCollapsed`; conditionally render category list

**Files:** `src/renderer/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`

---

## Verification
1. `npm run dev` — app launches

input.plan:
# Plan: Work Timer ("On the Clock")

## Context
Users with ADHD suffer from time blindness — they step away briefly and lose an hour. A simple clock-in/clock-out toggle with a live elapsed timer and persisted daily totals gives concrete feedback about time actually worked. Historical data (time per day) is stored for future display.

---

## Database

**New table: `work_sessions`**
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS work_sessions (
  id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  date       TEXT    NOT NULL,              -- ISO date "YYYY-MM-DD"
  started_at TEXT    NOT NULL,              -- ISO datetime
  ended_at   TEXT                           -- NULL while session is active
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_work_sessions_date ON work_sessions(date);
```
Added in `src/main/db/migrations.ts` (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS — safe to re-run).

---

## Backend

**`src/main/db/workSessions.ts`** (new file)
- `startSession(db)` — INSERT row with current datetime, return `{ id, startedAt }`
- `endSession(db, id)` — UPDATE `ended_at = now` for the given id
- `getTodayTotal(db)` — SELECT SUM of completed session durations for today's date; also add any active (unended) session up to now

**`src/shared/ipc-channels.ts`** — add `WORK_TIMER` group:
```ts
WORK_TIMER: {
  START:           'work-timer:start',
  STOP:            'work-timer:stop',
  GET_TODAY_TOTAL: 'work-timer:get-today-total',
}
```

**`src/main/ipc/workTimer.ts`** (new file) — register handlers for the three channels.

**`src/main/ipc/index.ts`** — call `registerWorkTimerHandlers()`.

---

## Frontend

**`src/renderer/src/lib/ipc.ts`** — add three typed wrappers:
- `startWorkSession(): Promise<{ id: number; startedAt: string }>`
- `stopWorkSession(id: number): Promise<void>`
- `getTodayWorkTotal(): Promise<number>` (total seconds)

**`src/renderer/src/hooks/useWorkTimer.ts`** (new file)
```ts
{
  isRunning: boolean
  sessionSeconds: number   // live elapsed seconds for current session
  todayTotalSeconds: number
  toggle: () => void       // clock in / clock out
}
```
- On mount: calls `getTodayWorkTotal()` to hydrate today's total
- On clock-in: calls `startWorkSession()`, stores `sessionId`, starts a `setInterval` tick
- On clock-out: calls `stopWorkSession(sessionId)`, clears interval, adds session to `todayTotalSeconds`

**`src/renderer/src/components/clock/WorkTimer.tsx`** (new file)
- A red pulsing circle button (Lucide `Circle` filled when on, outline when off)
- Shows live elapsed `H:MM:SS` while running
- Shows `Today: Xh Ym` below (from `todayTotalSeconds`)

**`src/renderer/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`** — render `<WorkTimer />` between the XP bar and the nav section (always visible when sidebar is open).

---

## Verification
1. `npm run dev`
2. Click the record button → turns red, timer ticks up
3. Click again → timer stops, "Today: Xh Ym" updates
4. Restart the app → "Today: Xh Ym" still shows the correct accumulated total
5. Check SQLite: `work_sessions` table has rows with `started_at` and `ended_at`

}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
// Typed wrappers around window.electron.ipcRenderer.invoke.
// The renderer imports from here — never calls ipcRenderer directly.

import { IPC } from '@shared/ipc-channels'
import type {
  Task,
  Category,
  CategoryWithCount,
  Subtask,
  CreateTaskPayload,
  UpdateTaskPayload,
  CreateCategoryPayload,
  UpdateCategoryPayload,
  CreateSubtaskPayload,
  UpdateSubtaskPayload,
  PomodoroSession,
  CompleteTaskResult,
  GamificationStats
} from '@shared/types'

const invoke = (channel: string, payload?: unknown) =>
  window.electron.ipcRenderer.invoke(channel, payload)

// ── Tasks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const getAllTasks = (opts?: {
  categoryId?: number
  includeComplete?: boolean
}): Promise<Task[]> => invoke(IPC.TASKS.GET_ALL, opts) as Promise<Task[]>

export const getTaskById = (id: number): Promise<Task | null> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.GET_BY_ID, { id }) as Promise<Task | null>

export const createTask = (payload: CreateTaskPayload): Promise<Task> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.CREATE, payload) as Promise<Task>

export const updateTask = (payload: UpdateTaskPayload): Promise<Task> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.UPDATE, payload) as Promise<Task>

export const deleteTask = (id: number): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.DELETE, { id }) as Promise<void>

export const reorderTasks = (orderedIds: number[]): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.REORDER, { orderedIds }) as Promise<void>

export const completeTask = (id: number): Promise<CompleteTaskResult> =>
  invoke(IPC.TASKS.COMPLETE, { id }) as Promise<CompleteTaskResult>

// ── Categories ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const getAllCategories = (): Promise<CategoryWithCount[]> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.GET_ALL) as Promise<CategoryWithCount[]>

export const createCategory = (payload: CreateCategoryPayload): Promise<Category> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.CREATE, payload) as Promise<Category>

export const updateCategory = (payload: UpdateCategoryPayload): Promise<Category> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.UPDATE, payload) as Promise<Category>

export const deleteCategory = (id: number): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.CATEGORIES.DELETE, { id }) as Promise<void>

// ── Subtasks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const getSubtasksByTask = (taskId: number): Promise<Subtask[]> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.GET_BY_TASK, { taskId }) as Promise<Subtask[]>

export const createSubtask = (payload: CreateSubtaskPayload): Promise<Subtask> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.CREATE, payload) as Promise<Subtask>

export const updateSubtask = (payload: UpdateSubtaskPayload): Promise<Subtask> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.UPDATE, payload) as Promise<Subtask>

export const deleteSubtask = (id: number): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.DELETE, { id }) as Promise<void>

export const reorderSubtasks = (taskId: number, orderedIds: number[]): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.SUBTASKS.REORDER, { taskId, orderedIds }) as Promise<void>

// ── Pomodoro ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const startPomodoroSession = (payload: {
  taskId?: number
  phase: PomodoroSession['phase']
  durationS: number
}): Promise<{ sessionId: number }> =>
  invoke(IPC.POMODORO.START_SESSION, payload) as Promise<{ sessionId: number }>

export const completePomodoroSession = (sessionId: number): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.POMODORO.COMPLETE_SESSION, { sessionId }) as Promise<void>

// ── App ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const minimizeToTray = (): Promise<void> =>
  invoke(IPC.APP.MINIMIZE_TO_TRAY) as Promise<void>
export const quitApp = (): Promise<void> => invoke(IPC.APP.QUIT) as Promise<void>

// ── Gamification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const getGamificationStats = (): Promise<GamificationStats> =>
  invoke(IPC.GAMIFICATION.GET_STATS) as Promise<GamificationStats>

Those are all harmless Chromium noise — nothing to do with your code. They happen because Electron tries to create a GPU disk cache on startup and Windows denies access to that temp folder (often because another Electron instance already has it locked, or it's a permissions quirk). The app still runs fine.

You can safely ignore them. If it bothers you seeing them, you can suppress the GPU cache errors by adding a flag to your app startup, but it's purely cosmetic — they have no effect on functionality.

Does the app itself look/work as expected when it opens?

input.content:
# Plan: Timer Persistence Fix + Pomodoro Task Completion

## Context
Two issues:
1. `WorkTimer` unmounts when the sidebar collapses (it's conditionally rendered), killing the hook state and stopping the clock.
2. Focus mode only shows the task title — user wants to complete the task and tick off subtasks without leaving focus mode, with auto-advance to the next task.

---

## Fix 1 — Timer survives sidebar collapse

**Root cause:** `useWorkTimer()` lives inside `<WorkTimer />`, which is only rendered when `!sidebarCollapsed`. Collapsing = unmount = hook destroyed.

**Fix:** Lift `useWorkTimer()` to `App.tsx`. Pass the four values (`isRunning`, `sessionSeconds`, `todayTotalSeconds`, `toggle`) down as props through `Sidebar` → `WorkTimer`.

- `src/renderer/src/hooks/useWorkTimer.ts` — no change
- `src/renderer/src/components/clock/WorkTimer.tsx` — convert from calling the hook itself to accepting props: `{ isRunning, sessionSeconds, todayTotalSeconds, toggle }`
- `src/renderer/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` — add four props to `SidebarProps`; pass them through to `<WorkTimer />`
- `src/renderer/src/App.tsx` — call `useWorkTimer()`, pass values to `<Sidebar />`

---

## Fix 2 — Task + subtask management inside Focus Mode

**What to add to `FocusMode.tsx`:**
- Task description (if present), shown below the title
- `<SubtaskList taskId={task.id} />` (reuse existing component — already handles toggle/add/delete/description)
- A "Mark Complete" button — calls `onComplete(task.id)`, then auto-advances to the next task

**Auto-advance logic:** `activeTasks` is passed to `FocusMode`. After completing, the current task is removed from that list. The next task is `activeTasks[0]` if it exists (since tasks are already sorted by `sort_order`). If none remain, call `exitFocusMode()`.

**Files:**
- `src/renderer/src/components/focus/FocusMode.tsx` — add `onComplete` prop, description display, `<SubtaskList />`, "Mark Complete" button, auto-advance
- `src/renderer/src/App.tsx` — pass `handleCompleteTask` and `activeTasks` to `<FocusMode />`

Note: `activeTasks` is already computed in App.tsx (`tasks.filter(t => !t.isComplete)`). `SubtaskList` is already fully functional and needs no changes.

---

## Verification
1. Clock in → collapse sidebar → re-expand → timer still running and elapsed time is correct
2. Enter focus mode on a task → description and subtasks visible
3. Tick a subtask checkbox → it marks complete inline
4. Click "Mark Complete" on the task → task moves to Done section, focus mode auto-advances to the next task (or exits if no tasks remain)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Plan: Work Timer ("On the Clock")

## Context
Users with ADHD suffer from time blindness — they step away briefly and lose an hour. A simple clock-in/clock-out toggle with a live elapsed timer and persisted daily totals gives concrete feedback about time actually worked. Historical data (time per day) is stored for future display.

---

## Database

**New table: `work_sessions`**
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS work_sessions (
  id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  date       TEXT    NOT NULL,              -- ISO date "YYYY-MM-DD"
  started_at TEXT    NOT NULL,              -- ISO datetime
  ended_at   TEXT                           -- NULL while session is active
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_work_sessions_date ON work_sessions(date);
```
Added in `src/main/db/migrations.ts` (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS — safe to re-run).

---

## Backend

**`src/main/db/workSessions.ts`** (new file)
- `startSession(db)` — INSERT row with current datetime, return `{ id, startedAt }`
- `endSession(db, id)` — UPDATE `ended_at = now` for the given id
- `getTodayTotal(db)` — SELECT SUM of completed session durations for today's date; also add any active (unended) session up to now

**`src/shared/ipc-channels.ts`** — add `WORK_TIMER` group:
```ts
WORK_TIMER: {
  START:           'work-timer:start',
  STOP:            'work-timer:stop',
  GET_TODAY_TOTAL: 'work-timer:get-today-total',
}
```

**`src/main/ipc/workTimer.ts`** (new file) — register handlers for the three channels.

**`src/main/ipc/index.ts`** — call `registerWorkTimerHandlers()`.

---

## Frontend

**`src/renderer/src/lib/ipc.ts`** — add three typed wrappers:
- `startWorkSession(): Promise<{ id: number; startedAt: string }>`
- `stopWorkSession(id: number): Promise<void>`
- `getTodayWorkTotal(): Promise<number>` (total seconds)

**`src/renderer/src/hooks/useWorkTimer.ts`** (new file)
```ts
{
  isRunning: boolean
  sessionSeconds: number   // live elapsed seconds for current session
  todayTotalSeconds: number
  toggle: () => void       // clock in / clock out
}
```
- On mount: calls `getTodayWorkTotal()` to hydrate today's total
- On clock-in: calls `startWorkSession()`, stores `sessionId`, starts a `setInterval` tick
- On clock-out: calls `stopWorkSession(sessionId)`, clears interval, adds session to `todayTotalSeconds`

**`src/renderer/src/components/clock/WorkTimer.tsx`** (new file)
- A red pulsing circle button (Lucide `Circle` filled when on, outline when off)
- Shows live elapsed `H:MM:SS` while running
- Shows `Today: Xh Ym` below (from `todayTotalSeconds`)

**`src/renderer/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`** — render `<WorkTimer />` between the XP bar and the nav section (always visible when sidebar is open).

---

## Verification
1. `npm run dev`
2. Click the record button → turns red, timer ticks up
3. Click again → timer stops, "Today: Xh Ym" updates
4. Restart the app → "Today: Xh Ym" still shows the correct accumulated total
5. Check SQLite: `work_sessions` table has rows with `started_at` and `ended_at`

content.text:
Clean. Here's what changed:

**Timer fix** — `useWorkTimer()` now lives in `App.tsx` and stays alive regardless of sidebar state. The hook's values are passed as props down through `Sidebar` → `WorkTimer`, so collapsing/expanding the sidebar no longer resets anything.

**Focus mode** — three additions:
- Task description shown below the title (if set)
- A "Subtasks" panel using the existing `SubtaskList` component — you can tick subtasks off without leaving focus mode
- A "Mark task complete" button at the bottom — completes the task, then auto-advances to the next task in your list (or exits focus mode if the list is empty)

file.content:
# ADHD Todo

A personal ADHD-friendly desktop to-do app built with Electron, React, TypeScript, and SQLite.

## Features

- **Categories** — always visible in sidebar (out of sight = forgotten)
- **DO BEFORE 5PM** banner with live countdown clock
- **Drag-to-reorder** tasks within a category
- **Recurring tasks** — daily, weekly, monthly (auto-creates next occurrence on completion)
- **Subtasks** and per-task time estimates
- **Pomodoro timer** — 4 × (25 min work + 5 min break) + 30 min long break
- **Focus mode** — full-screen overlay showing only the current task + Pomodoro (press Escape to exit)
- **Brain dump** — Ctrl+Space quick-add strip at the top of the main view
- **Gamification** — XP + level bar in sidebar, task completion animation, Pomodoro counter
- **System tray** — closing hides to tray; double-click tray icon to reopen; "Start on login" toggle
- Always dark mode

## Running the app

**Packaged (normal use):**
Double-click `launch.bat` in this folder. Creates no terminal window and falls back to dev mode if the exe isn't present.

The packaged exe lives at:
```
release\win-unpacked\ADHD Todo.exe
```

**Dev mode (hot reload):**
```bash
npm run dev
```

## Rebuilding the packaged exe

After making code changes:
```bash
npm run pack
```

> The command will print a `winCodeSign` symlink error at the very end — **ignore it**. The exe in `release\win-unpacked\` is fully built before that error occurs. It's an electron-builder quirk on Windows without Developer Mode enabled.

If you've reinstalled Node or switched Node versions, rebuild the native SQLite addon first:
```bash
npm run rebuild
npm run pack
```

## Data / backups

Your tasks and stats are stored in a SQLite database at:
```
%APPDATA%\adhd-todo\adhd-todo.db
```

To back up: copy that file somewhere safe. To restore: replace it with your backup before launching the app.

## Project structure

```
src/
  main/         — Electron main process (IPC handlers, SQLite, tray)
    db/         — Database layer (migrations, tasks, categories, gamification)
    ipc/        — IPC handler registration
  preload/      — contextBridge whitelist
  renderer/     — React app
    components/ — UI components
    hooks/      — Data-fetching hooks (useTasks, useCategories, useUserStats)
    store/      — Zustand ephemeral UI state (active category, focus mode)
    lib/ipc.ts  — Typed renderer-side IPC wrappers
  shared/       — Types and IPC channel constants shared across all processes
resources/
  icon.ico      — App icon (256×256 PNG-embedded ICO)
release/
  win-unpacked/ — Packaged app output (not committed to git)
launch.bat      — Double-click launcher (recommended)
launch.vbs      — Silent launcher (requires .vbs→wscript.exe file association)
```

## Auto-start on login

Right-click the system tray icon → **Start on login**. Uses the packaged exe path — only works correctly when running from `release\win-unpacked\`, not dev mode.

# ADHD Todo

A personal ADHD-friendly desktop to-do app built with Electron, React, TypeScript, and SQLite.

## Features

- **Categories** — always visible in sidebar (out of sight = forgotten)
- **Collapsible sidebar** — hide the whole left panel to see just your tasks and the clock
- **DO BEFORE 5PM** banner with live countdown clock
- **Drag-to-reorder** tasks within a category
- **Recurring tasks** — daily, weekly, monthly (auto-creates next occurrence on completion)
- **Subtasks** with per-subtask descriptions; inline tick-off from task card or focus mode
- **Task descriptions** — optional freeform notes on tasks and subtasks
- **Done section** — completed tasks persist in a collapsible "Done (N)" section at the bottom of the list
- **Pomodoro timer** — 4 × (25 min work + 5 min break) + 30 min long break
- **Focus mode** — full-screen overlay showing the current task, its description, subtasks, and Pomodoro timer; mark the task complete and auto-advance to the next one (press Escape to exit)
- **Brain dump** — Ctrl+Space quick-add strip at the top of the main view
- **Gamification** — XP + level bar in sidebar, task completion animation, Pomodoro counter
- **Work timer** — clock in/out button in the sidebar; live elapsed time and daily total persisted to the database
- **System tray** — closing hides to tray; double-click tray icon to reopen; "Start on login" toggle
- Always dark mode

## Running the app

**Packaged (normal use):**
Double-click `launch.bat` in this folder. Creates no terminal window and falls back to dev mode if the exe isn't present.

The packaged exe lives at:
```
release\win-unpacked\ADHD Todo.exe
```

**Dev mode (hot reload):**
```bash
npm run dev
```

## Rebuilding the packaged exe

After making code changes:
```bash
npm run pack
```

> The command will print a `winCodeSign` symlink error at the very end — **ignore it**. The exe in `release\win-unpacked\` is fully built before that error occurs. It's an electron-builder quirk on Windows without Developer Mode enabled.

If you've reinstalled Node or switched Node versions, rebuild the native SQLite addon first:
```bash
npm run rebuild
npm run pack
```

## Data / backups

Your tasks, stats, and work sessions are stored in a SQLite database at:
```
%APPDATA%\adhd-todo\adhd-todo.db
```

To back up: copy that file somewhere safe. To restore: replace it with your backup before launching the app.

## Project structure

```
src/
  main/         — Electron main process (IPC handlers, SQLite, tray)
    db/         — Database layer
                    migrations.ts      — schema creation and ALTER TABLE migrations
                    tasks.ts           — task CRUD + recurring logic
                    subtasks.ts        — subtask CRUD
                    categories.ts      — category CRUD
                    gamification.ts    — XP / level helpers
                    pomodoro.ts        — Pomodoro session logging
                    workSessions.ts    — work timer session logging
    ipc/        — IPC handler registration (one file per domain)
  preload/      — contextBridge whitelist
  renderer/     — React app
    components/ — UI components
      focus/    — FocusMode overlay + PomodoroTimer
      tasks/    — TaskCard, TaskList, TaskForm, SubtaskList
      clock/    — LiveClock, WorkTimer
      layout/   — Header, Sidebar
      ...
    hooks/      — Data-fetching and state hooks
                    useTasks.ts        — task list with optional completed-task inclusion
                    useSubtasks.ts     — subtask list with add/update/toggle/remove
                    useCategories.ts   — category list
                    useUserStats.ts    — XP / gamification stats
                    usePomodoro.ts     — Pomodoro timer state machine
                    useWorkTimer.ts    — clock-in/out state, persists sessions to DB
    store/      — Zustand ephemeral UI state (active category, focus mode, sidebar collapse)
    lib/ipc.ts  — Typed renderer-side IPC wrappers
  shared/       — Types and IPC channel constants shared across all processes
resources/
  icon.ico      — App icon (256×256 PNG-embedded ICO)
release/
  win-unpacked/ — Packaged app output (not committed to git)
launch.bat      — Double-click launcher (recommended)
launch.vbs      — Silent launcher (requires .vbs→wscript.exe file association)
```

## Auto-start on login

Right-click the system tray icon → **Start on login**. Uses the packaged exe path — only works correctly when running from `release\win-unpacked\`, not dev mode.

  shared/       — Types and IPC channel constants shared across all processes
resources/
  icon.ico      — App icon (256×256 PNG-embedded ICO)
release/
  win-unpacked/ — Packaged app output (not committed to git)
launch.bat      — Double-click launcher (recommended)
launch.vbs      — Silent launcher (requires .vbs→wscript.exe file association)
```

## Auto-start on login

Right-click the system tray icon → **Start on login**. Uses the packaged exe path — only works correctly when running from `release\win-unpacked\`, not dev mode.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ADHD Todo

A personal ADHD-friendly desktop to-do app built with Electron, React, TypeScript, and SQLite.

## Features

- **Categories** — always visible in sidebar (out of sight = forgotten)
- **DO BEFORE 5PM** banner with live countdown clock
- **Drag-to-reorder** tasks within a category
- **Recurring tasks** — daily, weekly, monthly (auto-creates next occurrence on completion)
- **Subtasks** and per-task time estimates
- **Pomodoro timer** — 4 × (25 min work + 5 min break) + 30 min long break
- **Focus mode** — full-screen overlay showing only the current task + Pomodoro (press Escape to exit)
- **Brain dump** — Ctrl+Space quick-add strip at the top of the main view
- **Gamification** — XP + level bar in sidebar, task completion animation, Pomodoro counter
- **System tray** — closing hides to tray; double-click tray icon to reopen; "Start on login" toggle
- Always dark mode

## Running the app

**Packaged (normal use):**
Double-click `launch.bat` in this folder. Creates no terminal window and falls back to dev mode if the exe isn't present.

The packaged exe lives at:
```
release\win-unpacked\ADHD Todo.exe
```

**Dev mode (hot reload):**
```bash
npm run dev
```

## Rebuilding the packaged exe

After making code changes:
```bash
npm run pack
```

> The command will print a `winCodeSign` symlink error at the very end — **ignore it**. The exe in `release\win-unpacked\` is fully built before that error occurs. It's an electron-builder quirk on Windows without Developer Mode enabled.

If you've reinstalled Node or switched Node versions, rebuild the native SQLite addon first:
```bash
npm run rebuild
npm run pack
```

## Data / backups

Your tasks and stats are stored in a SQLite database at:
```
%APPDATA%\adhd-todo\adhd-todo.db
```

To back up: copy that file somewhere safe. To restore: replace it with your backup before launching the app.

## Project structure

```
src/
  main/         — Electron main process (IPC handlers, SQLite, tray)
    db/         — Database layer (migrations, tasks, categories, gamification)
    ipc/        — IPC handler registration
  preload/      — contextBridge whitelist
  renderer/     — React app
    components/ — UI components
    hooks/      — Data-fetching hooks (useTasks, useCategories, useUserStats)
    store/      — Zustand ephemeral UI state (active category, focus mode)
    lib/ipc.ts  — Typed renderer-side IPC wrappers
  shared/       — Types and IPC channel constants shared across all processes
resources/
  icon.ico      — App icon (256×256 PNG-embedded ICO)
release/
  win-unpacked/ — Packaged app output (not committed to git)
launch.bat      — Double-click launcher (recommended)
launch.vbs      — Silent launcher (requires .vbs→wscript.exe file association)
```

## Auto-start on login

Right-click the system tray icon → **Start on login**. Uses the packaged exe path — only works correctly when running from `release\win-unpacked\`, not dev mode.

input.new_string:
## Getting launch.bat working after code changes

`launch.bat` checks for the packaged exe first. If it finds one it runs that — which won't have your latest changes. To get the batch file launching the updated app again you have two options:

**Option A — Rebuild the exe (recommended for normal use):**
```bash
npm run pack
```
After this, `launch.bat` will launch the freshly built exe with all your changes.

**Option B — Force dev mode (quickest for active development):**
Delete or rename the `release\win-unpacked\` folder. With no exe present, `launch.bat` falls back to `npm run dev` automatically, which always reflects the latest code.

---

# ADHD Todo

A personal ADHD-friendly desktop to-do app built with Electron, React, TypeScript, and SQLite.

## Features

- **Categories** — always visible in sidebar (out of sight = forgotten)
- **Collapsible sidebar** — hide the whole left panel to see just your tasks and the clock
- **DO BEFORE 5PM** banner with live countdown clock
- **Drag-to-reorder** tasks within a category
- **Recurring tasks** — daily, weekly, monthly (auto-creates next occurrence on completion)
- **Subtasks** with per-subtask descriptions; inline tick-off from task card or focus mode
- **Task descriptions** — optional freeform notes on tasks and subtasks
- **Done section** — completed tasks persist in a collapsible "Done (N)" section at the bottom of the list
- **Pomodoro timer** — 4 × (25 min work + 5 min break) + 30 min long break
- **Focus mode** — full-screen overlay showing the current task, its description, subtasks, and Pomodoro timer; mark the task complete and auto-advance to the next one (press Escape to exit)
- **Brain dump** — Ctrl+Space quick-add strip at the top of the main view
- **Gamification** — XP + level bar in sidebar, task completion animation, Pomodoro counter
- **Work timer** — clock in/out button in the sidebar; live elapsed time and daily total persisted to the database
- **System tray** — closing hides to tray; double-click tray icon to reopen; "Start on login" toggle
- Always dark mode

## Running the app

**Packaged (normal use):**
Double-click `launch.bat` in this folder. Creates no terminal window and falls back to dev mode if the exe isn't present.

The packaged exe lives at:
```
release\win-unpacked\ADHD Todo.exe
```

**Dev mode (hot reload):**
```bash
npm run dev
```

## Rebuilding the packaged exe

After making code changes:
```bash
npm run pack
```

> The command will print a `winCodeSign` symlink error at the very end — **ignore it**. The exe in `release\win-unpacked\` is fully built before that error occurs. It's an electron-builder quirk on Windows without Developer Mode enabled.

If you've reinstalled Node or switched Node versions, rebuild the native SQLite addon first:
```bash
npm run rebuild
npm run pack
```

## Data / backups

Your tasks, stats, and work sessions are stored in a SQLite database at:
```
%APPDATA%\adhd-todo\adhd-todo.db
```

To back up: copy that file somewhere safe. To restore: replace it with your backup before launching the app.

## Project structure

```
src/
  main/         — Electron main process (IPC handlers, SQLite, tray)
    db/         — Database layer
                    migrations.ts      — schema creation and ALTER TABLE migrations
                    tasks.ts           — task CRUD + recurring logic
                    subtasks.ts        — subtask CRUD
                    categories.ts      — category CRUD
                    gamification.ts    — XP / level helpers
                    pomodoro.ts        — Pomodoro session logging
                    workSessions.ts    — work timer session logging
    ipc/        — IPC handler registration (one file per domain)
  preload/      — contextBridge whitelist
  renderer/     — React app
    components/ — UI components
      focus/    — FocusMode overlay + PomodoroTimer
      tasks/    — TaskCard, TaskList, TaskForm, SubtaskList
      clock/    — LiveClock, WorkTimer
      layout/   — Header, Sidebar
      ...
    hooks/      — Data-fetching and state hooks
                    useTasks.ts        — task list with optional completed-task inclusion
                    useSubtasks.ts     — subtask list with add/update/toggle/remove
                    useCategories.ts   — category list
                    useUserStats.ts    — XP / gamification stats
                    usePomodoro.ts     — Pomodoro timer state machine
                    useWorkTimer.ts    — clock-in/out state, persists sessions to DB
    store/      — Zustand ephemeral UI state (active category, focus mode, sidebar collapse)
    lib/ipc.ts  — Typed renderer-side IPC wrappers
  shared/       — Types and IPC channel constants shared across all processes
resources/
  icon.ico      — App icon (256×256 PNG-embedded ICO)
release/
  win-unpacked/ — Packaged app output (not committed to git)
launch.bat      — Double-click launcher (recommended)
launch.vbs      — Silent launcher (requires .vbs→wscript.exe file association)
```

## Auto-start on login

Right-click the system tray icon → **Start on login**. Uses the packaged exe path — only works correctly when running from `release\win-unpacked\`, not dev mode.

---
name: User context
description: Who the user is and why they built this app
type: user
---

The user has ADHD and built this app specifically for their own daily use. Features are motivated by real ADHD challenges — time blindness (prompted the work timer and "before 5pm" banner), out-of-sight/out-of-mind (prompted always-visible sidebar), and task overwhelm (prompted focus mode). They mentioned a puppy as a concrete example of how time blindness manifests (stepping away "briefly" and losing an hour).

Frame suggestions in terms of ADHD usability, not general productivity.

---
name: Project snapshot
description: Stack, DB location, key quirks, and features as of 2026-03-23
type: project
---

**Stack:** Electron + React + TypeScript + SQLite (better-sqlite3) + Tailwind. IPC via contextBridge whitelist in preload.

**DB location:** `%APPDATA%\adhd-todo\adhd-todo.db`

**launch.bat quirk:** Checks for `release\win-unpacked\ADHD Todo.exe` first — if found, runs the packaged exe (which won't have new changes). Delete that folder or run `npm run pack` to get changes reflected.

**Features shipped as of 2026-03-23:**
- Categories, drag-to-reorder, recurring tasks
- Task + subtask descriptions
- Done section (completed tasks persist, collapsible)
- Collapsible sidebar (full panel hide/show)
- Focus mode with subtask management and "mark complete" + auto-advance
- Pomodoro timer (4×25/5 + 30 long break)
- Brain dump (Ctrl+Space)
- XP/level gamification
- Work timer (clock in/out, persisted to `work_sessions` table with date + start/end times — historical data ready for future display)
- System tray

**Why:** `work_sessions` table was designed to support a future "time worked per day" history view — schema is already in place.

input.content:
---
name: Pre-existing TypeScript errors were masked
description: The web typecheck was silently skipped whenever the node typecheck failed
type: feedback
---

The project's `typecheck` script runs `tsc -p tsconfig.node.json && tsc -p tsconfig.web.json`. If the node check fails (exit 1), the web check never runs. Several pre-existing renderer errors (Promise<unknown> casts in ipc.ts, missing payload fields, unused imports) were hidden behind a single node-side error. Once the node errors were fixed, ~25 web errors surfaced all at once.

**Why:** Not a mistake — just a gotcha with chained typecheck scripts.
**How to apply:** When fixing typecheck errors in this project, expect a second wave of renderer errors to appear after the main-process errors are cleared. Don't be surprised — fix them in batch.
